data.content:
李飞飞
Li Fei-Fei is a person who graduated from Princeton University, researched Tibetan medicine in Tibet, obtained a PhD from Caltech, and held assistant professor positions at UIUC and Princeton University.

普林斯顿大学
Princeton University is an educational institution where Li Feifei completed undergraduate studies and later served as an assistant professor.

data.entity_name: 伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校
data.content:
伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校
University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign is an organization where Li Fei-Fei served as an assistant professor in the Electrical Engineering Department.

加州理工学院
California Institute of Technology is an institution where Li Feifei entered in 2000 and obtained a doctoral degree.

助理教授
Assistant Professor is an academic position held by Li Feifei at multiple institutions.

电机系
Department of Electrical Engineering is a department within the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign where Li Feifei worked.

西藏
Tibet is a location where Li Fei-Fei conducted research on Tibetan medicine for one year.

博士学位
PhD Degree is an academic qualification obtained by Li Fei-Fei from Caltech.

藏药
Tibetan medicine is a field of study that Li Feifei researched after undergraduate graduation.

计算机科学系
Department of Computer Science is a department within Princeton University where Li Feifei worked.

Yian Zhao
Yian Zhao is an author of the paper affiliated with Baidu Inc and Peking University, contributing equally to the work.

DETR
An end-to-end object detector based on Transformer proposed by Carion et al., eliminating anchor and NMS components.<SEP>DETR refers to end-to-end Transformer-based detectors that eliminate NMS but suffer from high computational costs.<SEP>An end-to-end object detector discussed in the related work section as a foundational model.

DINO
DINO is a detector model used for comparison, specifically the DINO-R50 variant which RT-DETR outperforms.<SEP>DINO suggests mixed query selection to help better initialize queries.<SEP>Query initialization method discussed in Section 2.2.<SEP>An object detection model variant discussed in the related work section.

Jinman Wei
Jinman Wei is an author of the paper affiliated with Baidu Inc.

data.entity_name: School of Electronic and Computer Engineering
data.content:
School of Electronic and Computer Engineering
The School of Electronic and Computer Engineering is a department within Peking University located in Shenzhen.

China
China is the country where the affiliated organizations of the authors are based.

Baidu Inc
Baidu Inc is a technology company based in Beijing, China, employing most of the paper's authors.

YOLO
YOLO is a popular framework for real-time object detection known for its speed and accuracy trade-off but affected by NMS.

Jie Chen
Jie Chen is an author of the paper affiliated with Peking University and is the corresponding author.

Shangliang Xu
Shangliang Xu is an author of the paper affiliated with Baidu Inc.

Shenzhen
Shenzhen is the city in China where the School of Electronic and Computer Engineering of Peking University is located.

Wenyu Lv
Wenyu Lv is an author of the paper affiliated with Baidu Inc and serves as the project leader.

RT-DETR
A real-time end-to-end object detector that outperforms YOLO detectors and eliminates NMS post-processing.<SEP>RT-DETR is the first real-time end-to-end object detector proposed to address speed and accuracy dilemmas in object detection.<SEP>A real-time object detection model proposed in the paper, designed to improve accuracy and reduce computational redundancy.

Peking University
Peking University is an educational institution with a School of Electronic and Computer Engineering in Shenzhen, China.

Guanzhong Wang
Guanzhong Wang is an author of the paper affiliated with Baidu Inc.

Beijing
Beijing is the city in China where Baidu Inc is located.

Qingqing Dang
Qingqing Dang is an author of the paper affiliated with Baidu Inc.

Yi Liu
Yi Liu is an author of the paper affiliated with Baidu Inc.

NMS
Non-Maximum Suppression is an operation used to filter prediction boxes, sensitive to hyperparameters like confidence and IoU thresholds.<SEP>NMS is a process that negatively affects the speed and accuracy of YOLOs, which DETRs aim to eliminate.<SEP>NMS is a post-processing algorithm used to eliminate overlapping output boxes in object detection.<SEP>Non-Maximum Suppression, a technique analyzed for its impact within the RT-DETR framework.

Hybrid Encoder
Hybrid Encoder is an efficient encoder designed for RT-DETR to process multi-scale features by decoupling interactions.

COCO
COCO is a dataset used to evaluate the accuracy of RT-DETR, where it achieved 53.1% / 54.3% AP.<SEP>A dataset used to evaluate the performance of the RT-DETR model, specifically for accuracy metrics.

Real-Time Object Detection
Real-Time Object Detection is the research area focused on detecting objects quickly with applications like autonomous driving.

Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection
Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection is a method proposed to provide high-quality initial queries to the decoder in RT-DETR.

Transformer
Transformer is an architecture used in DETR for end-to-end object detection.<SEP>Transformer is the underlying architecture for DETR-based detectors.

Uncertainty-minimal Query Selection
Uncertainty-minimal Query Selection is a method proposed to provide high-quality initial queries to the decoder.<SEP>A specific technique discussed in the Introduction for improving model performance.

CNN
CNN is a type of neural network architecture used in YOLOv1 for object detection.<SEP>CNN-based architecture is commonly adopted by existing real-time detectors like YOLO.

Carion et al.
Researchers who first proposed the end-to-end detector based on Transformer called DETR.

Objects365
Objects365 is a dataset used for pre-training RT-DETR to achieve performance improvements.<SEP>Objects365 is a dataset used for pre-training the RT-DETR model to improve accuracy.

T4 GPU
T4 GPU is hardware used to measure the Frames Per Second of the RT-DETR model.<SEP>T4 GPU is the hardware used to test the speed of the NMS operation with TensorRT FP16.<SEP>Hardware used to measure the inference speed of the RT-DETR model during testing.

YOLO Detectors
A family of CNN-based one-stage object detectors widely used for real-time detection, requiring NMS post-processing.

Average Precision
A metric (AP) used to measure accuracy, affected by confidence and IoU thresholds.<SEP>Average Precision is a metric used to measure the accuracy of object detectors.<SEP>Metric used to measure accuracy in Table 1.

Zhu et al.
Researchers who proposed query selection for two-stage DETR to help better initialize queries.

DINO-Deformable-DETR-R50
A specific detector model compared against RT-DETR-R50 in accuracy and speed.

Frames Per Second
Frames Per Second is a metric used to measure the speed of object detectors.

Figure 2
A figure showing the number of boxes at different confidence thresholds.<SEP>Figure 2 intuitively reflects that NMS is sensitive to its hyperparameters via a bar graph.<SEP>Bar graph captioned 'The number of boxes at different confidence thresholds'.<SEP>A flowchart diagram titled 'Overview of our pipeline' found in the technical document.

Figure 1
Figure 1 illustrates the performance comparison of RT-DETR models against YOLO detectors.<SEP>A figure referenced in the Introduction section of the paper to illustrate performance or architecture.<SEP>A diagram in the paper showing a comparative analysis of different LLM's responses to misleading historical fact-checking queries.

Carion Et Al.
Carion Et Al. proposed the end-to-end detector based on Transformer called DETR.

YOLOv5
YOLOv5 is an anchor-based detector used for analyzing NMS execution time.<SEP>YOLOv5 is an anchor-based detector tested in the end-to-end speed benchmark.<SEP>Object detection model affected by NMS hyperparameters.

Deformable-DETR
Deformable-DETR accelerates training convergence with multi-scale features.<SEP>A variant of DETR discussed in the related work section covering end-to-end detectors.

RT-DETR-R50
RT-DETR-R50 is a specific model variant that outperforms DINO-Deformable-DETR-R50.<SEP>A specific variant of the RT-DETR model using ResNet-50 backbone, achieving specific performance metrics.

Zhu Et Al.
Zhu Et Al. proposed query selection for two-stage DETR.

Confidence Threshold
Confidence Threshold is a hyperparameter used in NMS to filter boxes with low scores.<SEP>Confidence threshold is a hyperparameter that filters prediction boxes and affects NMS execution time and accuracy.<SEP>Hyperparameter evaluated in Table 1 with values 0.001, 0.01, and 0.05.

IoU Threshold
IoU Threshold is a hyperparameter used in NMS to discard boxes with high overlap.<SEP>IoU threshold is a hyperparameter influencing the number of prediction boxes filtered and NMS execution time.<SEP>Hyperparameter evaluated in Table 1 with values 0.5, 0.6, and 0.8.

Encoder
Encoder is a component of the RT-DETR architecture that is scaled for performance.<SEP>A component of the RT-DETR architecture that was redesigned to reduce computational redundancy.

YOLOv8
YOLOv8 is an anchor-free detector used for analyzing NMS execution time.<SEP>YOLOv8 is an anchor-free object detector evaluated for accuracy and execution time in the study.<SEP>Object detection model affected by NMS hyperparameters.

YOLOv1
YOLOv1 is the first CNN-based one-stage object detector to achieve true real-time object detection.<SEP>An early version of YOLO discussed under the real-time object detectors subsection.

Decoder
Decoder is a component of the RT-DETR architecture adjusted for flexible speed tuning.

EfficientNMSPlugin
EfficientNMSPlugin is the plugin component named in the URL directory path.<SEP>EfficientNMSPlugin is a TensorRT plugin adopted for NMS post-processing involving multiple kernels.

EfficientNMS Kernel
EfficientNMS kernel is a specific component of the EfficientNMSPlugin whose execution time is reported.

COCO val2017
A benchmark dataset used to evaluate detector accuracy and calculate average execution time.

TensorRT
TensorRT is the software project identified in the repository path of the URL.<SEP>TensorRT is a deep learning inference optimizer providing the EfficientNMSPlugin for NMS operations.

YOLOv7
YOLOv7 is an anchor-based detector tested in the end-to-end speed benchmark.

NVIDIA
NVIDIA is the organization identified in the repository path of the URL.<SEP>NVIDIA is the organization associated with the TensorRT repository on GitHub.

Table 1
A table showing the effect of IoU and confidence thresholds on accuracy and NMS execution time.<SEP>Table titled 'The effect of IoU threshold and confidence threshold on accuracy and NMS execution time'.<SEP>A table listing the improvement of Kongzi model performance in various metrics by RL training.<SEP>A table titled 'Improvement of Kongzi model performance in various metrics by RL training' located on page 6.

End-to-end Speed Benchmark
End-to-end speed benchmark is a methodology established to compare the speed of various real-time detectors fairly.

PP-YOLOE
PP-YOLOE is an anchor-free detector tested in the end-to-end speed benchmark.

COCO Val2017
COCO val2017 is the benchmark dataset chosen to evaluate detector accuracy and calculate average execution time.

GitHub
GitHub is the hosting platform indicated by the domain name in the URL.<SEP>The platform hosting the TensorRT repository URL provided in the text.<SEP>GitHub is a platform referenced in the file path structure of the image file.

YOLOv6
YOLOv6 is an anchor-free detector tested in the end-to-end speed benchmark.

EfficientNMSFilter
EfficientNMSFilter is one of the multiple kernels involved in the EfficientNMSPlugin.

efficientNMSPlugin
efficientNMSPlugin is the specific plugin component identified in the directory path of the URL.

Release 8.6
Release 8.6 is the version identifier specified in the branch path of the URL.

RadixSort
RadixSort is one of the multiple kernels involved in the EfficientNMSPlugin.

Execution Time
Execution time measures the speed of the NMS operation and end-to-end inference.

Page 1 of the RT-DETR research paper manuscript containing the conclusion of the Introduction, a list of main contributions, and the beginning of the Related Work section. The text details RT-DETR performance metrics compared to YOLO and DETR models. The page follows a two-column academic format and ends with a sentence fragment continuing to page 2.

data.entity_name: RT-DETR Paper Page 2 - NMS Analysis and Speed Benchmark (image)
data.content: This image represents page two of the RT-DETR research paper, focusing on the limitations of YOLO detectors due to Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS). The page features Figure 2 illustrating box counts at varying confidence thresholds and Table 1 quantifying the trade-off between NMS hyperparameters, accuracy (AP), and execution time. Section 3 establishes an end-to-end speed benchmark to justify the need for RT-DETR. The content bridges related work on DETRs with the empirical analysis motivating the proposed real-time detector.

This image reference points to page 3 of a vision knowledge documentation collection stored in a local GitHub repository structure. The file path suggests it is part of a structured document system for knowledge retrieval purposes. Actual visual content cannot be determined without image access.

YOLOs
A family of object detection models used as a baseline for comparison against RT-DETR in the research.

Real-time Object Detectors
Category of object detection models including YOLOv1 and CNN-based architectures.

data.entity_name: DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
data.content:
DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
Research paper analyzing object detection models and their performance.<SEP>Research paper title discussing real-time object detection methods and comparing DETR-based models with YOLOs.

Introduction
The initial section of the research paper outlining the problem, contributions, and redesign of the encoder.

108 FPS
Speed metric achieved by RT-DETR-R50 on the T4 GPU.

LaTeX
Typesetting system used to format the research paper with standard academic layout.

53.1% AP
Accuracy metric achieved by RT-DETR-R50 on the COCO dataset.

Flexible Speed Tuning
A feature supported by RT-DETR allowing for adjustable inference speeds.

Subsection 2.1
A section of the paper titled 'Real-time Object Detectors' discussing YOLOv1 and CNN architectures.

Subsection 2.2
A section of the paper titled 'End-to-end Object Detectors' discussing DETR variants.

End-to-end Object Detectors
Category of object detection models including DETR and its variants.

Related Work
A section of the paper reviewing existing object detection architectures and variants.

Section 2.2
Section titled 'End-to-end Object Detectors' discussing query initialization methods.

Section 3.1
Subsection titled 'Analysis of NMS' detailing hyperparameter impacts.

Conditional DETR
Query initialization method discussed in Section 2.2.

CNN-based Architectures
A category of neural network architectures discussed under Real-time Object Detectors.

Non-Maximum Suppression
Algorithm analyzed for its impact on detector speed and accuracy.

Section 3.2
Subsection titled 'End-to-end Speed Benchmark' outlining methodology for speed comparison.

Section 3
Section titled 'End-to-end Speed of Detectors' which introduces the analysis of detector speed.

NMS Execution Time
Metric used to measure speed in milliseconds in Table 1.

Page 3 Image File
Page 3 Image File is a specific image file identified by a local path, potentially representing page 3 of a document collection.

Speed Comparison
Methodology for fair speed comparison outlined in Section 3.2.

Page 2
The specific page of the research paper displayed in the image and analyzed.

Visual Analysis
Visual Analysis is a technique attempted on the image file which failed due to system access restrictions.

Image Content Analysis
Image Content Analysis is a report section detailing the examination results and metadata of an image file.

Local Path
Local Path refers to a file storage location type that cannot be accessed by the system, preventing analysis.

Knowledge Documentation System
Knowledge Documentation System is a system inferred from the file path structure, hosting vision-related documents.

Footnotes
Footnotes are metadata fields associated with the image content analysis, reported as None.

Captions
Captions are metadata fields associated with the image content analysis, reported as None.

Analyzer System
Analyzer System is the software system attempting to perform the visual analysis but unable to access the local path.

Historical Domain
The Historical Domain is characterized by high information density and strict requirements for factual accuracy.

Kongzi
Kongzi is a large language model specifically designed for historical understanding and complex reasoning.<SEP>A model evaluated under different judges and compared with other models like Deepseek-r1 and GPT-4o.<SEP>Kongzi is a language model specifically designed for the historical domain.<SEP>Kongzi is a historical large language model with fact enhancement capabilities described in the research paper.<SEP>A language model proposed in the paper for the historical domain, evaluated in the table.<SEP>Kongzi is a historical large language model designed to address factual inaccuracies in historical reasoning.

CoT Distillation
A method showing that high-quality CoT data can significantly boost model performance.

Reinforcement Learning
A process where the factuality score is used as a reward signal to train the model.<SEP>A learning method that optimizes performance through feedback-driven learning.<SEP>Reinforcement Learning is a method used to incentivize reasoning capability in LLMs according to the DeepSeek-R1 paper.<SEP>A technique used to enhance the effectiveness of the Kongzi language model.<SEP>A machine learning process illustrated in the flowchart where the HFRM operates.<SEP>A specific area of Machine Learning, including RLHF, covered in the cited works.<SEP>A training stage abbreviated as RL, utilizing the GRPO framework.

DeepSeek-R1
A competitive model utilizing RL with Group Relative Policy Optimization algorithm.<SEP>DeepSeek-R1 is a paper titled 'Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning' published on arXiv.

o1
A model demonstrating general reasoning capabilities that other methods fall short of.

Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
An existing method to enhance LLMs by aligning model outputs with human preferences.

O1
O1 is a model demonstrating general reasoning capabilities that other methods fall short of.

Reward Function
A mechanism used in RL training to assess model output quality through weighted aggregation.<SEP>A novel reward function that incorporates factual rewards into the reinforcement learning framework.<SEP>A mechanism used in training, integrating multiple reward components via weighted aggregation.

Historical Question Answering
A task used for extensive evaluations to demonstrate Kongzi's performance.

Chain-of-Thought
A reasoning method used in examples for structured instruction following during the second stage of SFT.<SEP>Chain-of-Thought is a fine-tuning technique that enhances reasoning capabilities by generating intermediate reasoning steps.<SEP>A format used to construct structured instruction-tuning training data from contextual reasoning texts.<SEP>A technique used for examples during the fine-tuning stage.

DeepSeek-AI
DeepSeek-AI is the organization associated with the research team and the DeepSeek-R1 model.<SEP>DeepSeek-AI is cited in the context of recent advancements in large language models.<SEP>DeepSeek-AI is an organization listed in the references associated with DeepSeek models.<SEP>The organization associated with the DeepSeek-R1 model.<SEP>An organization cited in the reasoning in large language models section of the related work.

Continued Pre-training
A training step where a base model is adapted to the historical domain using domain-specific corpora.

Graph of Relevant Information
A graph of entities and relations built to measure similarity to ground truth for the reward function.

Counterfactual Reasoning
A task used for extensive evaluations to demonstrate Kongzi's performance.

Fact-Enhanced Reinforcement Learning Strategy
A novel strategy introduced by Kongzi to improve factual consistency and reasoning depth.

Prompt Engineering
An early reasoning approach relying on carefully designed prompts to guide models.

Large Language Models
Large Language Models have achieved significant progress in natural language understanding and complex reasoning tasks.<SEP>Large Language Models are the subject of multiple papers in the text, including DeepSeek-R1 and surveys on hallucination.<SEP>A key topic covered in the citations listed in the bibliography, also referred to as LLMs.<SEP>A topic related to AI and LLMs discussed in the bibliography entries.

Instruction-following Fine-tuning
A fine-tuning process performed via a cold-start strategy using carefully constructed instruction data.

Historical Corpora
Domain-specific corpora related to history collected for continued pre-training.

Group Relative Policy Optimization
An algorithm used by DeepSeek-R1 for reinforcement learning.

Fact-Aware RL Framework
A novel framework evaluating the accuracy of each generated segment for factual correctness.

Large Pretrained Language Models
Models explored for multimodal understanding and generation within Chinese cultural contexts.

FoodieQA Dataset
A dataset introduced for multimodal understanding within Chinese cultural contexts.

Timeline Ito Games
A component of the CTM Benchmark used to assess model abilities.

Chinese History
The primary source of historical text data selected for continuity and completeness.

Chain-of-Thought Data
Data incorporated to facilitate the model's reasoning capacity in a standardized format.

Li et al.
Researchers who introduced the FoodieQA dataset.

Supervised Command Fine-Tuning
A training technique used to provide models with preliminary command understanding and execution capabilities.<SEP>A method to optimize the response structure and enable reasoning capabilities.

CTM Benchmark
A benchmark assessing abilities in cross-dynastic entity ordering, event alignment, and reasoning.

Fuxi
A systematic assessment framework considering Chinese cultural authenticity.

Continued Pre-Training
A training stage where the model obtains relevant historical information.<SEP>A contribution method listed for the Kongzi model involving ongoing model training.

Content RL
Reinforcement learning employed to optimize output quality.

Wadhwa, Amir, and Wallace
Researchers associated with CoT distillation study in 2024.

Wang et al.
Researchers who proposed the CTM benchmark.<SEP>Authors cited in the reasoning in large language models section of the related work.

Zhao et al.
Researchers who proposed the Fuxi framework.

Chain-Of-Thought Data
High-quality data introduced to enable models to acquire reasoning capabilities through preset templates.

SFT Method
A method used to enable models to generate text according to preset Chain-Of-Thought templates.

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
A strategy used to address hallucinations caused by insufficient information through external knowledge bases.

Historical Fact-Enhanced Reward Module (HFRM)
A novel component designed to capture alignment between generated text and historical facts by constructing knowledge graphs.

Reinforcement Learning Training
A training strategy aimed at enhancing the reasoning capabilities of language models.

Generalized Reward Policy Optimization (GRPO)
An optimization framework used to optimize policy model parameters by minimizing expectation and introducing regularization.

Language Models
Systems capable of generating text that are prone to factual errors or hallucinations in long-form generation.

Ground Truth Knowledge Graph
A knowledge graph curated by human experts used as a reference for alignment and evaluation.

data.entity_name: Factuality-Oriented Reward Mechanism For Historical Entities
data.content:
Factuality-Oriented Reward Mechanism For Historical Entities
A proposed mechanism built upon the GRPO framework to reinforce factual accuracy and relational completeness.

Ground Truth Graph
A reference graph curated by human experts used to compare with candidate knowledge graphs for evaluation.

Knowledge Graph
A structure built by extracting entities and relations from generated text to serve as the foundation for information representation.<SEP>A structured representation of information generated to correspond with content and aligned with a ground truth graph.

KL Divergence
A regularization term used to constrain the difference between new and old policies in the objective function.

Policy Model
The model whose parameters are optimized by the objective function to enhance factual consistency.<SEP>A component in the reinforcement learning process that takes input 'q' and generates a sequence of outputs.

Human Experts
Individuals who curate the ground truth knowledge graph for semantic similarity calculation.

Twenty-Four Histories
Publicly available historical sources included in the Chinese historical text corpus.

Historical Accuracy
Historical Accuracy is a metric evaluating the accuracy of statements regarding real historical events.<SEP>An evaluation metric component contributing to the model's response process score.<SEP>A criterion used in the results evaluation to assess factual correctness in history.<SEP>A specific performance metric listed in Table 1.

Problem Solving
Problem Solving is a metric evaluating whether the problem is solved normally.<SEP>An evaluation metric component contributing to the model's response process score.<SEP>A criterion used in the results evaluation to assess solution generation.<SEP>A specific performance metric listed in Table 1.

Logical Reasoning
Logical Reasoning is a metric evaluating whether the logic of reasoning is correct.<SEP>An evaluation metric component contributing to the model's response process score.<SEP>A criterion used in the results evaluation to assess logical capabilities.<SEP>A specific performance metric listed in Table 1.

Classical Chinese
The original language of most texts in the corpus, requiring preprocessing for training.

data.entity_name: Chinese Historical And Cultural Annotated Text Corpus
data.content:
Chinese Historical And Cultural Annotated Text Corpus
A collection of nearly 200 million tokens of Chinese historical and cultural annotated text.

Qwen2.5
The base model with 0.5B and 7B parameters used for the experiments.

Factual Statement Data
A category of the corpus containing objective descriptions of historical events and relationships.

NVIDIA A6000 GPU
Hardware used for continued pretraining of the 0.5B and 7B models.

Contextual Reasoning Text
A category of the corpus containing inferable value through contextual relationships and metaphors.

Vernacular Chinese
A language style used alongside Classical Chinese for generating question-answer pairs.

Large Language Model
A tool employed to automatically generate question-answer pairs and identify historical events.<SEP>The general category of AI models to which Kongzi belongs.

NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 GPU
Hardware used for supervised fine-tuning on augmented data.

Supervised Fine-Tuning
A training stage conducted after continued pre-training to achieve human alignment.<SEP>Supervised Fine-Tuning is a procedure detailed in the experiments section.<SEP>A training stage abbreviated as SFT, involving fine-tuning on Chain-of-Thought examples.

Gemini-2.5
An AI model listed in the performance comparison table.<SEP>A model listed in the comparison table alongside Kongzi variants.<SEP>A base model used for performance comparison against the Kongzi models.

GPT-4o
GPT-4o is a mainstream LLM selected to score model responses according to the scoring template.<SEP>A model listed in the comparison table alongside Kongzi variants.<SEP>GPT-4o serves as one of the judges for evaluating model scores.<SEP>A base model used for performance comparison against the Kongzi models.

Continued PreTraining
A training stage performed on base models using cleaned classical Chinese data.<SEP>Continued PreTraining is a procedure detailed in the experiments section.

Kongzi-0.5B-SFT
A smaller version of the Kongzi AI model trained using Supervised Fine-Tuning.<SEP>A Kongzi model variant with 0.5B parameters after supervised fine-tuning.

Kongzi-0.5B-RL
A smaller version of the Kongzi AI model optimized with Reinforcement Learning.<SEP>A Kongzi model variant with 0.5B parameters after reinforcement learning.

Factuality Score
A metric derived from semantic similarity between graphs that serves as a reward signal.

Kongzi-7B-SFT
A larger version of the Kongzi AI model trained using Supervised Fine-Tuning.<SEP>A Kongzi model variant with 7B parameters after supervised fine-tuning.

GRPO
The reinforcement learning framework adopted for optimizing the Kongzi models.<SEP>A framework specified for use in the Reinforcement Learning Training section.

Deepseek-r1
A model used as a sample baseline for comparing Kongzi's response performance.<SEP>Deepseek-r1 is an AI model used as a baseline for comparison.

Qwen-Max
Qwen-Max is a mainstream LLM selected to score model responses.<SEP>An AI model listed in the performance comparison table.<SEP>Qwen-Max serves as one of the judges for evaluating model scores.<SEP>A base model used for performance comparison against the Kongzi models.

SFT Training
Supervised Fine-Tuning process conducted in two stages using general and augmented datasets.

Knowledge Graphs
Structures extracted from model output and ground truth to calculate factual rewards via embedding.<SEP>Structures created from information extraction to represent nodes connected by lines.<SEP>Structures used in the reward function calculation based on cosine similarity.

Kongzi-7B-RL
A version of the Kongzi AI model optimized with Reinforcement Learning, demonstrating significant performance improvements over SFT versions.<SEP>A Kongzi model variant with 7B parameters after reinforcement learning.

RL Training
Reinforcement Learning process used to optimize model performance after SFT completion.<SEP>RL Training refers to the reinforcement learning process applied to Kongzi models.

Model Output
The content generated by the Kongzi models during the evaluation process.

Ground Truth
Reference data used to evaluate the accuracy of the model output.<SEP>Data used in the Reward Model sub-process for comparison against generated content.

Factual Reward
A reward component based on the cosine similarity of knowledge graphs between output and ground truth.<SEP>Factual Reward is a component of the reward formula representing factual accuracy.

Format Reward
A specific reward function checking for correct format markers like begin_of_thought.<SEP>Format Reward is a component of the reward formula representing format adherence.<SEP>A reward component checking for specific markers like begin_of_thought.

Repetition Penalty Reward
A specific reward function penalizing repeated n-grams based on repetition ratio.<SEP>Repetition Penalty Reward is a component of the reward formula penalizing repetition.<SEP>A reward component penalizing repeated n-grams.

Augmented Dataset
A dataset split and used across both stages of SFT training.

Logical Coherence Reward
A reward component assessing the logical flow of the model generation.<SEP>Logical Coherence Reward is a component of the reward formula representing logical consistency.

Kongzi-7B (RL)
Kongzi-7B (RL) is the reinforcement learning trained version of the Kongzi 7B model.

Deepseek r1
Deepseek r1 is a reasoning model listed in the Reasoning Model Table.<SEP>A Large Language Model included in the performance comparison table.

General SFT Dataset
A dataset used in the first stage of SFT training alongside half of the augmented dataset.

Deepseek r1 distill 7B
Deepseek r1 distill 7B is a distilled version of the Deepseek r1 model.

Kongzi-0.5B
Kongzi-0.5B is a small-parameter version of the Kongzi model.<SEP>Kongzi-0.5B is an AI model evaluated in the technical document.<SEP>A specific smaller version of the Kongzi model compared in Table 1.

Kongzi-7B
Kongzi-7B is an AI model that received 72.13% support from volunteers and achieved the highest score in the evaluation.<SEP>Kongzi-7B is a version of the Kongzi model.<SEP>Kongzi-7B is an AI model evaluated in the technical document.<SEP>A specific larger version of the Kongzi model compared in Table 1.

Kongzi-0.5B (RL)
Kongzi-0.5B (RL) is the reinforcement learning trained version of the Kongzi 0.5B model.

Qwen3-235B-a33B
Qwen3-235B-a33B is a mixed reasoning model.

Qwen2.5-72B
Qwen2.5-72B is a dense model listed in the Base Model Table.

Deepseek r1 distill 14B
Deepseek r1 distill 14B is a distilled version of the Deepseek r1 model.

CMMLU-Chinese-History
CMMLU-Chinese-History is a historical benchmark dataset used for evaluation.<SEP>A benchmark dataset used to evaluate the performance of language models in Chinese history.

Reward Formula
The Reward Formula calculates the final reward using weighted components.

Graph-Enhanced Reinforcement Learning
Graph-Enhanced Reinforcement Learning is a method adopted to reduce hallucinations and improve reasoning ability.

SFT
SFT refers to the Supervised Fine-Tuning version used as a baseline for comparison.

OpenAI
OpenAI is the organization associated with the GPT-4o model.<SEP>OpenAI is an organization listed as an author entity alongside numerous researchers in the text.

Results Evaluation
Results Evaluation is the process conducted using manual expert scoring and LLM scoring to ensure fairness.<SEP>A section in the paper detailing the methodology for scoring model performance.

Cold-Start Training
Cold-Start Training is a process conducted to align the model with the question-answering format.

Kongzi 0.5B
Kongzi 0.5B is a smaller version of the Kongzi model that showed astonishing capabilities.

CMMLU
CMMLU is a publicly available Chinese historical benchmark used as a test set for evaluation.

Gemini-2.0-flash-lite-preview
Gemini-2.0-flash-lite-preview is a mainstream LLM selected to score model responses.<SEP>Gemini-2.0-flash-lite-preview serves as one of the judges for evaluating model scores.

Cosine Similarity
A mathematical method used to compute the reward signal based on knowledge graph embeddings.<SEP>A metric used to compare embeddings from Ground Truth and Generation paths to determine semantic alignment.

Answer Score
Answer Score is a metric evaluating the quality of the answer result.<SEP>A criterion used in the results evaluation to assess model answers.<SEP>A specific performance metric listed in Table 1.

Deepseek-R1
Deepseek-R1 is an LLM model that was surpassed by the Kongzi 7B model in performance.

Think Score
Think Score is a metric evaluating the thinking ability or strategy of the model.<SEP>A criterion used in the results evaluation to assess model thinking.<SEP>A specific performance metric listed in Table 1.

LLM-as-a-Judge
LLM-as-a-Judge is a method or concept surveyed in the paper by Gu, J. et al.

Huang, L.
Huang, L. is the first author of the survey on Hallucination in Large Language Models.

This image is the first page of the research paper introducing 'Kongzi,' a large language model optimized for historical accuracy. It features the abstract and introduction text alongside Figure 1, which illustrates the model's capability to detect and correct factual hallucinations in historical narratives compared to other LLMs. The figure uses a comparative layout to show how misleading prompts can lead to errors and how Kongzi addresses this through structured fact-checking.

This image captures a page from the 'Kongzi' research paper, detailing the methodology for creating a historical large language model. It outlines the model's core contributions—continued pre-training, CoT data generation, and fact-aware reinforcement learning—and provides specific technical details on data acquisition, including the processing of 0.2 billion tokens of Chinese historical text. The content bridges the gap between related work in reasoning models and the specific implementation strategies used to enhance factual accuracy in historical domains.

data.entity_name: Machine Learning Pipeline Overview and Training Strategy Page (image)
data.content: This image presents Figure 2 and accompanying text from a research paper describing a multi-stage machine learning pipeline. The workflow involves data cleaning, augmentation, and Chain-of-Thought (CoT) generation, followed by Continue Pre-Training (CPT), two-stage Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), and Content Reinforcement Learning (RL). The text elaborates on data generation strategies, initial training to enhance reasoning capabilities, and a reinforcement learning approach using a Historical Fact-enhanced Reward Module (HFRM) to reduce hallucinations.

data.entity_name: HFRM Architecture and GRPO Objective Function Diagram (image)
data.content: This image presents Figure 3 from a technical paper, illustrating the Historical Fact-enhanced Reward Module (HFRM). The diagram visualizes a pipeline where a Policy Model generates text that is evaluated against Ground Truth using knowledge graph embeddings and cosine similarity. The accompanying text defines the mathematical framework for Generalized Reward Policy Optimization (GRPO), emphasizing the use of semantic similarity as a reward signal to improve factual accuracy in AI models.

data.entity_name: Kongzi Model Performance Chart and Experimental Methodology Page (image)
data.content: This image shows Figure 4 comparing Kongzi model variants against other LLMs under three judge models, alongside detailed experimental methodology including dataset curation, preprocessing, and multi-stage training protocols using classical Chinese historical corpora and GPU infrastructure.

data.entity_name: Kongzi Model Performance Metrics and RL Framework Documentation (image)
data.content: This image contains a page from a research paper detailing the performance improvements of the 'Kongzi' AI models (0.5B and 7B parameters) achieved through Reinforcement Learning (RL) training. It features a comprehensive data table comparing RL-trained models against SFT baselines across metrics like logical reasoning and problem solving when evaluated against GPT-4o, Gemini-2.5, and Qwen-Max. The text further elaborates on the GRPO reinforcement learning framework, defining specific reward functions for factual accuracy, formatting, and repetition penalties used to optimize the model's output.

data.entity_name: Kongzi Model Performance Evaluation and Conclusion Page (image)
data.content: This image captures page 7 of a research paper introducing 'Kongzi,' a language model designed for historical domains. It features Table 2, presenting benchmark results on CMMLU-Chinese-History comparing Kongzi against models like Qwen and Deepseek. The text details the evaluation methodology involving human and LLM scoring and concludes with a summary of the model's achievements through reinforcement learning and cold-start training, followed by a list of references.

data.entity_name: Academic Bibliography Page on Large Language Models (image)
data.content: This image captures page 8 of a research paper's reference list, featuring a dense compilation of citations focused on recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs). It includes technical reports and surveys from major AI entities such as OpenAI, DeepSeek, and Qwen, covering topics like reasoning capabilities, hallucination, and preference optimization, with publication dates ranging primarily from 2023 to 2025.

data.entity_name: Academic Bibliography Page on AI and LLMs (image)
data.content: This image captures a bibliography page listing ten academic references focused on advanced machine learning topics such as Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), Chain-of-Thought reasoning, and Large Language Model alignment. The citations range from 2017 to 2025 and include sources from NeurIPS conferences and arXiv preprints, indicating a research context involving state-of-the-art AI methodologies.

Fact-Enhanced Reinforcement Learning
A strategy integrated into Kongzi to improve historical reasoning accuracy.

Kongzi Paper
An anonymous academic submission titled 'Kongzi: A Historical Large Language Model with Fact Enhancement.'

5 Ws of Fact-Checking
A framework including Who, What, Where, When, Why, arranged around a central Event/Fact node.

Entity-Relation Graphs
Used to create a novel reward function for the Kongzi model.

Battle of Changban
A historical event used as a query example in the comparative analysis of LLM responses.

CoT Data Generation
A contribution method for generating Chain-of-Thought data for the Kongzi model.

In Three Kingdoms
A source compared against 'Official History' regarding events like Cao Cao beheading Xing Daorong.

Cao Cao
A historical figure mentioned in the context of beheading Xing Daorong.

Ancient Chinese History Texts
A collection of texts totaling 0.2 billion tokens selected for data acquisition in the method section.

Xing Daorong
A character involved in the Battle of Changban, identified as fictional in 'Romance of the Three Kingdoms.'

Official History
A source of historical record compared against 'In Three Kingdoms' regarding factual discrepancies.

CTM
A benchmark referenced in the related work section regarding Chinese history in large language models.

Academic Research Paper
The document titled Kongzi: A Historical Large Language Model with Fact Enhancement, formatted in a two-column conference proceedings layout.

Romance of the Three Kingdoms
A literary work where Xing Daorong is identified as a fictional character.

Fact-Aware RL
A contribution method utilizing Fact-Aware Reinforcement Learning for the Kongzi model.

Plaat et al.
Authors cited in the reasoning in large language models section of the related work.

Machine Learning Pipeline
A machine learning pipeline detailed in a technical document, involving data cleaning, augmentation, and training stages.

Factual Statements
A category of historical corpora separated during the classification workflow using an LLM-assisted process.

Original Corpus
The initial input data feeding into the data cleaning module of the pipeline.

Data Cleaning
A module that processes the original corpus into factual data and inference text.

Small-Parameter LLM
A tool used in the LLM-assisted process to classify historical corpora into factual and reasoning texts.

Technical Document
A research paper containing the machine learning pipeline description and mathematical equations.<SEP>The Technical Document contains the experimental results and methodology.<SEP>The source document containing the analysis and Table 1.

Contextual Reasoning Texts
A category of historical corpora separated during the classification workflow using an LLM-assisted process.

Factual Data
Data derived from the original corpus after cleaning, used for augmentation.

Inference Text
Text derived from the original corpus after cleaning, used for chunking and generation.

CoT Data (Chain-of-Thought)
Data generated from inference text, used for supervised fine-tuning.

SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning)
A training stage that receives concatenated CPT output and CoT data.

Data Augmentation
A process applied to factual data before continue pre-training.

Historical Fact-enhanced Reward Module
HFRM is a module detailed in a research paper that uses factuality scores to mitigate hallucinations in text generation.

Content RL (Reinforcement Learning)
The final training node in the pipeline following SFT.

Generate
A step that creates Chain-of-Thought data from chunked inference text.

Chunking
A step applied to inference text to prepare it for generation.

Reference Model
A model that processes outputs generated by the Policy Model within the HFRM framework.

Generation
Generated content processed in parallel with Ground Truth within the Reward Model.

Mathematical Equations
Equations labeled (1) and (2) defining variables found in the text sections.

Reward Model
A model that produces reward values based on processed outputs and internal mechanisms involving ground truth and generation.

CPT (Continue Pre-Training)
A training node that processes augmented factual data.

Embedding
Representations derived from knowledge graphs used for semantic alignment comparison.

Information Extraction
A process step converting paths into knowledge graphs within the Reward Model mechanism.

Generalized Reward Policy Optimization
GRPO is an optimization method with an objective function defined in the academic text associated with HFRM.

O3-mini
O3-mini is an AI model included in the performance comparison.

Long-form Text Generation
A task where hallucinations occur and is the target application for the HFRM.

Hallucinations
Errors in long-form text generation that the HFRM aims to mitigate using factuality scores.

Deepseek-r1-distill
Deepseek-r1-distill is a variant AI model family evaluated in the chart.

QwQ-32B-preview
QwQ-32B-preview is an AI model included in the performance comparison.

Chinese Historical Texts
Chinese Historical Texts comprise 200 million tokens used for dataset construction.

NVIDIA GPUs
NVIDIA GPUs are hardware used for training procedures.

A6000
A6000 is a specific GPU model used for training.

RTX 3090
RTX 3090 is a specific GPU model used for training.

Base Model Training
Base Model Training is a procedure detailed in the experiments section.

Figure 4
Figure 4 is a bar chart comparing AI model scores under different judges.

Kongzi Model
An AI model family whose performance improvement is evaluated in the document.

Research Paper
The source document containing the technical diagram and academic text describing the HFRM.

Deepseek-r1 Ratio
A specific performance metric listed in Table 1.

Page 6
The specific page of the technical document displayed in the image.

Technical Research Paper
The document analyzed, specifically page 7, containing research findings on language models.

Table 2: Results On CMMLU-Chinese-History
A table in the paper comparing performance of Large Language Models on a specific benchmark.

Chain Of Thought

Conclusion
